title: triplophyllum fraternum subsp. biokoense
intro: Triplophyllum fraternum subsp. biokoense is a rare and endemic subspecies of plant found in Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea, belonging to the family Hymenophyllaceae, commonly known as the filmy ferns. The plant is traditionally used in local medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities. The health benefits of Triplophyllum fraternum subsp. biokoense include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and promote wound healing, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often used to make infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are believed to possess therapeutic properties that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
